questions.question: A recipe for baking bread requires $2 \cdot 3.75$ cups of flour to make $15$ loaves. What is the unit rate of flour per loaf of bread?
If a water tank is being filled at a rate of $4.2 \cdot 10^2$ liters per minute, how many liters will it hold after $15$ minutes?
The cost of a construction project varies directly with the number of workers. If a project that requires $18$ workers costs $$ 36000$, what is the cost per worker?
A cake recipe calls for $2 \frac{1}{4}$ cups of sugar for every $2$ cakes. What is the unit rate of sugar per cake?
The volume of a rectangular prism varies directly with its length. If a prism with a length of $8$ feet has a volume of $48$ cubic feet, what is the volume of a prism with a length of $12$ feet?
The speed of a car is $45 \cdot 10^3$ meters per $4$ hours. What is the unit rate of distance per hour?
A bakery sells $480$ loaves of bread per day. If each loaf requires $0.75$ cups of flour, how many cups of flour are used in a day?
A pipe can fill a tank at a rate of $12$ cubic feet per minute. If the tank has a capacity of $2.4 \cdot 10^3$ cubic feet, how many minutes will it take to fill the tank?

A construction project requires $15$ workers to complete $3$ units of work in $8$ hours. If the number of workers is increased to $20$, how many hours will it take to complete $5$ units of work? Show all work and explain your reasoning.

A chef is making a sauce that requires a ratio of $2:3$ of two ingredients. If the chef has $12$ cups of one ingredient, how many cups of the other ingredient are needed? If the amount of one ingredient is increased to $18$ cups, how will the amount of the other ingredient change? Use mathematical models to support your answer.

teacher_rules: Read each question carefully before answering.
Use a pencil to fill in the answer circles.
Check your work before submitting your test.
